question_title: What will be the price of Bitcoin (BTC) on July 10, 2026?
summary: Bitcoin closes ~$58,620 on July 1, 2026, resolving July 10 (9-day horizon). Downtrend regime with mild bearish drift; Monte Carlo median ~$57,300, wide fat-tailed distribution reflecting crypto volatility and crisis-cascade downside risk.

factors.description: Current price July 1 close ~$58,620; horizon 9 days (status quo anchor)
Bearish momentum: broke below $60K support flipped to resistance, extreme fear (index 15), 20-day drift -0.44%/day
Oversold bounce potential; drift estimate very noisy (SE~0.37%/day)
Analyst downside targets $44-53K if support fails; crisis regime fat tails

reasoning:
Forecast submitted.

**Bitcoin price on July 10, 2026 — numeric forecast:**

- **Median: ~$57,300** (down slightly from July 1 close of $58,620)
- **10th–90th percentile: $52,400 – $62,800**
- **P1: $47,800 | P99: $68,600**

**Reasoning:** BTC closed at ~$58,620 on July 1, 2026, giving a 9-day horizon. The market is in a clear downtrend — it broke below $60K key support (now resistance), the Fear & Greed Index sits at extreme fear (15), and 20-day drift is -0.44%/day. I built a Monte Carlo simulation from 29 days of empirical returns (daily vol ~2.0-2.2%), using a mildly bearish drift center (dampened from the noisy raw estimate) with wide parameter uncertainty and Student-t(4) fat tails to capture crypto's leverage-cascade downside risk (analysts cite $44-53K targets if support fails) and oversold-bounce upside. Both tails are wider than a pure random walk to reflect the active crisis regime.
sources_consulted: [Bitcoin USD (BTC-USD) Price History & Historical Data - Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/BTC-USD/history/)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the closing price of Bitcoin (BTC) in US dollars for July 10, 2026, as reported in the "Close" column of CoinGecko's Bitcoin historical data table at https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/bitcoin/historical_data . For example, a close of $61,250 resolves as 61250. The relevant value is the row dated 2026-07-10.
fine_print: CoinGecko reports daily close prices in USD; the value used is whatever CoinGecko publishes for the 2026-07-10 row at resolution. If CoinGecko's historical data page is unavailable or does not report a value for that date, the daily close price reported by CoinMarketCap (https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/bitcoin/historical-data/) for July 10, 2026 will be used instead. If neither source reports a value, the question resolves based on the BTC/USD close reported by other credible sources (https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) for July 10, 2026 (UTC).

factors.description: Current price July 1 close ~$58,620; horizon 9 days. Status quo anchor.
Bearish momentum: 20-day drift -0.44%/day, 10-day -0.76%/day, broke below $60K support flipped to resistance, extreme fear (index 15)
Oversold bounce potential; drift estimate noisy (SE~0.37%/day), could mean-revert upward
Analyst downside targets $44-53K if support fails; crisis regime fat tails

input.anchor: Random walk from current $58,620 with empirical ~2% daily vol over 9 days
input.assessment: Monte Carlo from 29 days of empirical returns. Daily vol ~2.0-2.2%, drift center mild negative (-0.25%/day) with parameter uncertainty, student-t(4) fat tails. 9-day horizon. Median ~$57,300 (slight bearish tilt from $58,620), P10 $52,400, P90 $62,800. Downside tail (P1 $47,800) captures crisis-cascade risk; upside limited over 9 days.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether bearish momentum continues or oversold bounce occurs; magnitude of drift over 9 days is highly noisy.
input.tool_audit: web_search returned excellent structured Yahoo Finance daily history and current price. execute_code ran Monte Carlo cleanly. No failures.
input.process_reflection: Straightforward short-horizon crypto price question. The empirical-data + Monte Carlo path worked well. No friction.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
Verified all four factors against the research trace; the forecast is well-grounded and the probability distribution is calibrated.

GROUNDING CHECKS (all pass):
- Factor 1 ($58,620 current close): Yahoo Finance current_price 58619.99. ✓
- Factor 2 (bearish momentum): "20-day drift -0.44%/day" matches code output (29-day mean -0.4455%, Yahoo trailing_mean_return_20d -0.4041%); "10-day -0.76%/day" matches code `last10 mean -0.758`; "$60K support flipped to resistance" matches financemagnates result ("weekly close below $60,000 flipped this year's key support into fresh resistance"); "extreme fear index 15" matches changelly result. ✓
- Factor 3 (oversold bounce / noisy drift): supported by parameter-uncertainty in the Monte Carlo (SE reasoning) and the reversal narrative in KuCoin/phemex results. ✓
- Factor 4 (analyst downside $44-53K): matches financemagnates result ("25% drop to $44,100... $53,700 the first support shelf"). ✓

DIRECTION CHECKS: Factor logits are all sign-consistent (bearish -0.6, bounce +0.4, downside -0.4, anchor 0). No wrong-direction factors, no double-counting (momentum / mean-reversion / tail / anchor are distinct).

REGIME WINDOW: The 29-day return window (June 2–July 1) is a single coherent downtrend with no structural break, so drift/vol estimates are not regime-contaminated. Notably the agent *dampened* the observed -0.44%/day drift to a -0.25%/day center with wide parameter uncertainty — a conservative, appropriate adjustment rather than naively extrapolating recent bearishness.

RESOLUTION ALIGNMENT: Criteria specify CoinGecko's daily close; agent used Yahoo Finance BTC-USD closes as a proxy. These track within a fraction of a percent, acceptable slack for a 9-day-ahead numeric forecast.

PROBABILITY: My independent estimate matches the agent's. Random-walk-with-slight-bearish-tilt from $58,620 over 9 days at ~2% daily vol yields median ≈$57,300, ~10th/90th at roughly $52K/$63K — exactly what the agent produced. Student-t(4) fat tails appropriately capture the leverage-cascade downside risk highlighted in the sources.

SELF-EXAMINATION: The counterargument is genuinely two-sided (oversold-relief-rally to $63-65K vs. leverage cascade below $50K), each with evidentiary basis from the trace, not a token gesture. Confidence 0.70 is well-calibrated for a volatile 9-day crypto horizon.

TOOL AUDIT: Agent claims "no failures"; ground truth confirms 0/7 errors. Consistent. The narrative omits mentioning the 2 stock_price calls but makes no false claim about them.

No material errors found.
